summary: Justin Brennan teaches how to build a capital foundation before closing deals by establishing business identity, creating a CRM database of 30 contacts, and executing a mock deal (pitch deck, landing page, Loom video, email campaign) to warm up investors and shift how people perceive you as a multifamily investor rather than just your primary profession.
takeaways: Change email signature from 'Realtor Justin' to 'Realtor and Multifamily Investor' with multifamily website link to shift market perception
Build CRM with minimum 30 people you know (not necessarily wealthy) and use bulk CSV import with name/email/phone
Create mock deal marketing package: pitch deck + 5-10 minute Loom video summary + dedicated landing page + email to database asking 'who do you know' not asking for money
Mock deal email template: subject 'quick look [unit count] unit deal,' tone 'I'm closing on it with or without you,' include returns/highlights/video link/pitch deck, ask referrals not investments
Send mock deal email asking 'Can you send this to anyone you may know?' rather than 'Do you have $50k to invest?'
Pull mock deal after 3 weeks with message: 'Found issues during escrow, pulling away, thanks for interest, will send next deal'
Follow SEC guidance: avoid posting specific deal terms on social media; acceptable to send deal details via email to known contacts or warm introductions (506b compliant)

skill_candidates.change: Systematically warm capital before closing real deals using mock deal process
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin explains mock deal serves dual purpose: (1) marketing exercise showing professional deal packaging, (2) warming database so people understand your multifamily investor identity before asking for real capital commitments

skill_candidates.change: Identify markets via three-state Crexy pull and broker conversations to narrow based on 'pull' signals
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin tells Brian: 'take three states, get on Crexy and pull everything 10-50 units, dial for dollars with brokers, narrow based on conversation pull'

skill_candidates.change: Accept and project dual identity (current profession + multifamily investor) to change how others perceive you
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin changed email signature to include 'multifamily investor,' which shifted client perception from 'commodity realtor' to differentiated investor, resulting in unsolicited investor inquiries and partner referrals

title: Tuesday Mentorship Q&A - How to Build a Capital Foundation_06/09/26| Justin Brennan
